context: Tests whether the agent writes structured logging calls using a static string as the first argument and a hash of structured fields as the second argument, avoids string interpolation in the log message, and includes an `event:` key for ops dashboard integration.

checklist.name: Static first argument
checklist.description: Every Rails.logger call uses a static string literal as its first argument — no string interpolation (e.g., no `"Processing #{id}"`) in the first argument of any logger call

checklist.name: Hash second argument
checklist.description: At least two Rails.logger calls pass a hash as the second argument containing key-value pairs of structured data (e.g., `{ order_id: ..., user_id: ... }`)

checklist.name: Dynamic data in hash only
checklist.description: Dynamic values (IDs, amounts, statuses, names) appear exclusively inside the hash argument — NOT interpolated or concatenated into the first string argument

checklist.name: event: key present
checklist.description: At least one logger call includes an `event:` key in its hash argument (e.g., `event: "payment.processed"` or `event: "payment.failed"`)

checklist.name: Domain-specific hash fields
checklist.description: The log hash(es) include domain-relevant field names such as `payment_id:`, `user_id:`, `amount_cents:`, or `status:` — not just generic keys like `data:` or `info:`

checklist.name: Multiple log levels
checklist.description: Uses at least two distinct logger severity levels (e.g., `logger.info` and `logger.error`) to distinguish normal events from error conditions

checklist.name: No interpolation in any logger call
checklist.description: No logger call in the file uses Ruby string interpolation (`#{}`) or string concatenation (`+`) in the message argument — zero occurrences

checklist.name: At least four log statements
checklist.description: The service contains at least four distinct Rails.logger calls, covering different stages (e.g., start, success, failure, and at least one intermediate step)

checklist.name: Failure path logged at error level
checklist.description: The failure or error path uses `Rails.logger.error` (or `.warn`) — NOT `Rails.logger.info` — for the failure condition

checklist.name: No puts or p for logging
checklist.description: The service does NOT use `puts`, `p`, or `pp` to output log information — all logging goes through Rails.logger
